turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing a persistent productive cough for the past 5 days, and it's started to get worse with yellowish-green sputum. I'm also having trouble breathing and wheezing, which is making me really anxious.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: I've got this persistent productive cough that just won't go away - it started out dry but now it's producing yellowish-green sputum. It's been going on for five days, and I'm really worried about it because it's getting worse every day.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago with a dry cough, but over the past few days, it has become productive with yellowish-green sputum.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all the time now - day and night, I'm coughing up this yucky green stuff. It's really getting on my nerves because I just can't seem to shake it off no matter what I do!

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 or 9 out of 10 - it's constant and really disrupting my life. I'm tired all the time, can't sleep because of the coughing at night, and just feel miserable overall.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yes, I've also been experiencing difficulty breathing and wheezing, which is making me really anxious. And sometimes I get a low-grade fever that's around 38.2°C (100.8°F), mostly in the evenings. It's like my body is just trying to tell me something's wrong!

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yes, the coughing and wheezing get much worse when I'm exerting myself or breathing deeply - even just taking a few steps can make it feel like my lungs are burning. It's really frustrating because I love working out and being active, but now it feels like a chore because I know it'll trigger these awful symptoms.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been having night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for 4 nights now, which is really unsettling. And to be honest, I did have some fatigue and malaise that's made me miss three days of work - I just feel so drained and overwhelmed by this whole thing!

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been experiencing right-sided chest pain that's sharp and worsens with deep breathing and coughing. It's a pretty intense feeling that makes me worry something serious is going on inside my body.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My legs and feet feel fine, but to be honest, I've been so focused on this cough and breathing issues that I haven't even noticed if there's any swelling or not.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season for my seasonal allergies, but it hasn't helped with this cough at all. Additionally, I've been taking OTC dextromethorphan (cough suppressant) for the past week, but it's only provided minimal relief - I still have a persistent productive cough and other symptoms.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My last physical exam about a year ago showed everything was normal, and I've never been diagnosed with anything serious before.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I haven't had my cholesterol checked recently, but that's not really relevant to what's going on right now. I'm more concerned about this persistent cough and difficulty breathing than any potential issues with my cholesterol levels.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I don't smoke at all. As for drinking, I do have a glass of wine on weekends, maybe 1-2 glasses. But that's it, really. I'm not concerned about that being related to my symptoms, though - this just feels like something more serious going on with my lungs or respiratory system.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: Honestly, I've been feeling pretty lethargic lately and haven't had much of an appetite, so I've probably not been eating as well as I should be. As for exercise, I'm an elementary school teacher, so I'm on my feet all day teaching and interacting with the kids - that's my main form of physical activity. But recently, even just walking to class has felt exhausting because of this persistent cough and breathing issues.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mother actually has asthma, which I've always thought was interesting given my current symptoms. I'm not aware of any other heart-related conditions in my immediate family that I know about.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer, but aside from that, I don't think there's anything else notable.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been about a year since my last physical exam. Everything seemed normal back then, and I've never had any serious health concerns before this persistent cough and breathing issues started.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin - it causes a rash on my skin, so I try to avoid taking that kind of medication whenever possible.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been working with young children all day at school and they've had some upper respiratory infections lately, which makes me wonder if it's contagious. And I just found out that several students in my class have been sick with similar symptoms to mine, so I'm getting a bit worried about the possibility of something spreading around.
